name: Curtis Mayfield
abstract: Curtis Lee Mayfield (June 3, 1942 – December 26, 1999) was an American singer-songwriter, guitarist, and record producer. Dubbed the Gentle Genius, he is considered one of the most influential musicians of soul and socially conscious African-American music.

works_created.t: I Just Can't Work No Longer

about_quotes.speaker: Buckingham
about_quotes.evidence: He said people would be singing along with it, like the Impressions he was talking about, Curtis Mayfield and © Fillius Jazz Archive -16- the Impressions.

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: Coming from Chicago, there was groups like the Impressions, Curtis Mayfield, Jerry Butler, you know the Chicago groups that were there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Freeman interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Don Alias
about_quotes.evidence: Curtis Mayfield, you know if you listen to the soundtrack of “Superfly,” and you know you’ll hear some conga drums and stuff in there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Don Alias interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002
